notes: Launching XP version works, as choosing the first version causes a black screen after language selection. Feels sluggish.
title: X-COM: UFO Defense

notes:
Tried on decade old centrino laptop, YMMV. On Dos version, Game shows black screen  with a laggy mouse cursor.
XP version shows file Open dialog box. Tried opening the two exes, one show black screen , other errors out with "Patch code time out"

Thats when i noticed that the packaged game is just a dosboxed version. So 'apt install dosbox' and running command 'dosbox' in the game folder and the game works flawlessly (It uses the dosbox config file in the folder). No lag, no issues whatsoever.  So 'runs perfectly after tweaks' if said tweak is sidestepping Steam play completely. ¯\_(ツ)_/¯
title: X-COM: UFO Defense

notes: Worked out of the box, was able to start a new campaign. Everything seems to work audio, mouse controls etc.
title: X-COM: UFO Defense

notes:
Sounds play with a slight delay(compared to the "real" DOS version of the game).
Like clicking a button will sound half a second later. Also the intro "video" shows this delay between firering a weapon and the weapon sound itself is delayed.
title: X-COM: UFO Defense

notes: Use 'steam-dos' to play this game perfectly. https://github.com/dreamer/steam-dos
title: X-COM: UFO Defense

notes: I have tried many old and new Proton versions, all of them eventually result in crashing. This might be because this is a DOS game and Wine has been removing their DOS support. This game might run better if you try running it in the Linux version of DOSBox instead of via Proton.
title: X-COM: UFO Defense
